source.title: Hierarchical predictive coding in distributed pain circuits

confidence_rationale: Comprehensive mini-review (2023) explicitly formalizing two anatomically separable ascending pain pathways within a hierarchical predictive coding framework: medial pathway (dACC + AIC, affective-emotional) and lateral pathway (somatosensory cortex, sensory-discriminative). The predictive coding formalization with precision-weighted PEs at each level maps directly to SD-011's z_harm_s (lateral, forward-predictable via PE) vs z_harm_a (medial, integrated affective state). The posterior-to-anterior insular gradient with traveling waves provides an implementation mechanism. Marked as a mini-review rather than primary experimental data.
failure_signatures: This is a mini-review with a proposed framework, not primary experimental validation. Many components remain speculative (as the author acknowledges).
The medial/lateral pathway distinction maps to cortical processing, not directly to the spinal-level Adelta/C-fiber distinction that SD-011 grounds its dual streams in.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Two major ascending pain pathways are anatomically and functionally separable. The medial pain pathway involves dACC and AIC as main nodes, whereas the lateral pain pathway involves somatosensory cortex. Within a hierarchical predictive coding framework, signals descending the hierarchy convey predictions while signals ascending the hierarchy propagate prediction errors. The precision-weighted gain parameter (relative precision xi_2/(xi_2+xi_1)) modulates PE magnitude at each level. The insula shows a functional gradient: PIC encodes stimulus intensity and expectations (prediction units), AIC responds to prediction errors as modality-unspecific aversive surprise signals.
mapping.ree_translation: Lateral pathway (somatosensory cortex, PIC) = z_harm_s: encodes sensory-discriminative information, subject to predictive coding cancellation via precision-weighted PEs. When E2_harm_s accurately predicts harm_s_next, the PE is small and the gain attenuates the signal. Medial pathway (dACC, AIC) = z_harm_a: encodes affective-emotional/motivational state, integrates across modalities and time, generates the 'aversive surprise' signal that drives behavioral control. The cingulate-insula hub coordinates these streams hierarchically rather than as parallel independent channels. This matches SD-011's claim that z_harm_s is forward-predictable (amenable to E2 forward model) while z_harm_a is integrative/motivational (feeds E3 directly).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The medial/lateral cortical pathway distinction does not perfectly map to the spinal Adelta/C-fiber distinction that SD-011 uses as its biological grounding. The cortical pathways receive mixed input from both fiber types. Chen's framework emphasizes the cingulate-insula hub as a coordinating center, suggesting the streams are not fully independent but hierarchically organized — this could either strengthen or complicate SD-011's clean two-stream separation depending on how the hierarchy is interpreted.
mapping.source_context: Mini-review integrating human neuroimaging, rodent optogenetics, and computational theory. Proposed framework with some speculative components. 2023 publication reflects current state of the field.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality moderate (Frontiers mini-review, not primary data, but comprehensive and well-grounded in recent optogenetics evidence). Mapping fidelity high: the explicit two-pathway formalization with precision-weighted PEs maps very directly to SD-011's dual-stream architecture. Transfer risk very low: directly about the computational architecture of human pain processing, synthesizing both human and animal evidence.
